Tool_water_bill_Description: Estimate water charges with kl slabs and a fixed meter charge. Illustrative city tariff.
Tool_water_bill_LearnText: Municipal water is often billed in kilolitre (kl) slabs — the first block is cheaper, higher usage costs more per kl. Enter consumption and slab rates (defaults are illustrative) to estimate the bill.
Tool_water_bill_FormulaNote: Illustrative only. Real bills add sewerage, meter rent, and city-specific slabs. Check your municipal tariff card.
Tool_water_bill_Slab1Hint: First column: upper kl limit. Second: rate ₹/kl.
Tool_water_bill_Slab2Hint: Second slab ends at this kl limit.
Tool_water_bill_Slab3Hint: All kl above slab 2 at this rate.
Tool_water_bill_Step_3: Charge each band of kl at its rate.
Tool_water_bill_Step_4: Add fixed charge for total bill.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Purpose: Estimate a domestic water bill using simple multi-slab rates in kl.
Tool_water_bill_Info_WhyKnow: Understanding slabs shows why cutting high-band usage saves more than the first cheap kl.
Tool_water_bill_Info_RealLife: Monthly household bill check, summer usage planning, comparing municipal tariffs.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit the bill into overall expenses.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Inflation_When: To see how rising tariffs erode purchasing power.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_When: Saving the difference from lower usage.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_Example: Redirect savings to an emergency fund.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows a clear per-slab kl breakdown plus fixed charge.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Motto: The last kilolitres cost the most — use slabs wisely.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Descriptio: Estimate savings interest from balance, rate (~2.7–3%), and days — with Section 80TTA note.
Tool_savings_account_interest_LearnText: Banks credit savings interest on daily / average balance. Enter balance, annual rate, and days to estimate interest. Section 80TTA exempts up to ₹10,000 of savings interest for non-seniors (educational).
Tool_savings_account_interest_FormulaNot: Simplified daily product. Real banks may use min-balance or product methods. Seniors may use 80TTB (₹50,000) instead of 80TTA.
Tool_savings_account_interest_RateHint: Typical savings rates are about 2.7–3% p.a. — check your bank.
Tool_savings_account_interest_VerdictOk: Interest {0} is within the ₹10,000 Section 80TTA exemption (illustrative, non-senior).
Tool_savings_account_interest_VerdictTax: Interest {0}; about {1} may be taxable above 80TTA (illustrative).
Tool_savings_account_interest_ScenarioSu: Same balance at 2.7%, your rate, and 3%
Tool_savings_account_interest_ExampleSte: Compare 2.7% vs 3% in the scenario cards.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Purpo: Estimate interest on a savings balance and see how it relates to Section 80TTA.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_WhyKn: Idle cash earns little; knowing interest and tax exemption helps park money wisely.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_RealL: Comparing bank savings rates, parking emergency funds, tax planning for interest income.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Alt_F: When you can lock money for higher rates.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Alt_B: To decide how much to keep liquid.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Alt_T: To see overall tax on interest income.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Diffe: FinYatra pairs simple interest math with a clear 80TTA exemption note.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Motto: Know what your idle cash earns — and what 80TTA covers.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Description: Estimate SGB returns: 2.5% annual interest on issue price plus capital gains at redemption (educational).
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_LearnText: Sovereign Gold Bonds pay 2.5% p.a. on the issue price for 8 years (paid semi-annually). At redemption you get the prevailing gold price. Capital gains on maturity for individuals are typically tax-free — this tool is educational.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_FormulaNote: Illustrative. Issue price and redemption price vary by tranche. Interest is taxable; maturity capital gains for individuals are generally exempt.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_YearsHint: SGB tenure is 8 years (exit window from year 5 on interest payment dates).
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_RedeemHint: Use an assumed future gold price to see capital gains education.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_VerdictNote: Invested {0}; interest {1}; capital gain {2}. Maturity gains for individuals are generally tax-free (educational).
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_ScenarioSubtitl: Flat gold vs your assumption vs +20%
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Step_2: Set tenure (default 8) and assumed redemption price.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Purpose: Estimate interest and capital gains on Sovereign Gold Bonds.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_WhyKnow: SGB combines gold price exposure with a fixed 2.5% coupon and favourable tax on maturity.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_RealLife: Comparing SGB vs physical gold or gold ETF for long-term holding.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Alt_Gold_W: For digital/physical gold without SGB coupon.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Alt_Fd_Whe: For fixed rupee returns without gold risk.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows SGB coupon plus redemption gain scenarios in one view.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Motto: Gold price plus 2.5% — know both sides of SGB.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Description: Senior FD maturity at higher rates with TDS note (₹50k threshold) and comparison vs regular FD.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Subtitle: Higher rate FD + senior TDS note
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_LearnText: Banks often offer ~0.5% extra on senior citizen FDs. Enter deposit, senior rate, and tenure to see maturity. TDS applies if interest exceeds ₹50,000 for seniors (illustrative).
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_FormulaNote: Quarterly compounding by default. TDS is simplified; Form 15H may avoid TDS if eligible. Compare also with SCSS.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_RateHint: Often ~0.5% above regular FD — check your bank.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_SeniorHint: Affects TDS threshold only; rate is what you enter.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_VerdictOk: Net maturity {0}. Interest below TDS threshold in this estimate.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_VerdictTds: Net maturity {0} after estimated TDS of {1}.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_ExampleStep_3: Interest = M − P; compare with regular rate (r − 0.5%).
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Step_4: Apply TDS note if above threshold.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_Abbreviation: Senior citizen FD — higher rate + TDS
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_Purpose: Estimate senior FD maturity and compare with a regular rate.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_WhyKnow: The senior rate bump compounds meaningfully over multi-year tenures.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_RealLife: Parking retirement corpus, comparing bank senior FD vs SCSS.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_Goal_1: See net maturity at senior rate
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra highlights the senior rate bump vs regular FD plus TDS threshold.
Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_Motto: That extra 0.5% compounds — check TDS too.
Tool_term_insurance_Description: Estimate life cover need via income×years or expense method — and the gap vs existing cover.
Tool_term_insurance_Subtitle: Income or expense method → cover gap
Tool_term_insurance_LearnText: Choose income replacement (income × years) or expense coverage (expenses × years), add liabilities, and subtract existing cover to see the gap. This is a need estimator — not a premium quote.
Tool_term_insurance_FormulaNote: Educational only. Advisors may use HLV or other methods. Premiums depend on age, health, and insurer.
Tool_term_insurance_LiabilitiesHint: Home loan, personal loans, and other debts to clear.
Tool_term_insurance_Result_GapOk: Existing cover meets or exceeds need
Tool_term_insurance_VerdictOk: Suggested cover {0} — existing cover looks adequate in this estimate.
Tool_term_insurance_ScenarioSubtitle: Income vs expense vs higher of the two
Tool_term_insurance_ExampleStep_5: Compare with expense method in scenarios.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much term life cover you may need and any gap.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_WhyKnow: Under-insurance leaves dependents exposed; over-insurance wastes premium.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_RealLife: Buying first term plan, reviewing cover after a home loan, annual insurance check.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Goal_1: Estimate cover by income or expenses
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_When: For medical cover need, not life cover.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Alt_Goal_When: For education / retirement goals alongside cover.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra compares income and expense methods and shows the cover gap clearly.
Tool_term_insurance_Info_Motto: Cover the gap — income or expenses, then liabilities.
Tool_nri_tax_Description: Rough tax note on India income by residential status (Resident / RNOR / NRI) — simplified slabs, educational.
Tool_nri_tax_Subtitle: Residential status + India income → rough tax
Tool_nri_tax_LearnText: Residential status decides what India taxes. NRIs and RNORs are generally taxed on India-sourced income; residents on worldwide income. Enter status and India income for a simplified new-regime-style estimate — not DTAA advice.
Tool_nri_tax_FormulaNote: Educational approximation. DTAA, TDS rates, and special NRI rules can change the outcome. Consult a CA for filing.
Tool_nri_tax_StatusHint: Based on days in India under Income-tax Act — simplified labels here.
Tool_nri_tax_IncomeHint: Salary in India, rent, capital gains, interest, etc. (illustrative total).
Tool_nri_tax_VerdictNote: As {0}, estimated tax on India income ≈ {1}.
Tool_nri_tax_VerdictResident: Residents are generally taxed on worldwide income — only India income is modelled here.
Tool_nri_tax_VerdictNri: NRI/RNOR: typically India-sourced income is taxed in India; foreign income usually is not (subject to exceptions / DTAA).
Tool_nri_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: Same India income under NRI / RNOR / Resident
Tool_nri_tax_ExampleStep_3: If income ≤ ₹12 lakh, rebate may make tax nil in this model.
Tool_nri_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare the same income under RNOR and Resident labels.
Tool_nri_tax_Step_3: Estimate tax with simplified slabs + cess.
Tool_nri_tax_Step_4: Read scope note for foreign income.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_Purpose: Give a rough tax estimate on India income by residential status.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Status mistakes are common and change what India can tax.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_RealLife: Returning NRIs, rent from Indian property, NRE/NRO interest planning.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For full resident old vs new regime compare.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra frames tax by residential status with a clear India vs foreign income note.
Tool_nri_tax_Info_Motto: Status first — then tax the right income.
